NKJV.1: A Contemplation of David. A Prayer when he was in the cave. I cry out to the LORD with my voice; With my voice to the LORD I make my supplication.
NKJV.2: I pour out my complaint before Him; I declare before Him my trouble.
NKJV.3: When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, Then You knew my path. In the way in which I walk They have secretly set a snare for me.
NKJV.4: Look on my right hand and see, For there is no one who acknowledges me; Refuge has failed me; No one cares for my soul.
NKJV.5: I cried out to You, O LORD: I said, “You are my refuge, My portion in the land of the living.
NKJV.6: Attend to my cry, For I am brought very low; Deliver me from my persecutors, For they are stronger than I.
NKJV.7: Bring my soul out of prison, That I may praise Your name; The righteous shall surround me, For You shall deal bountifully with me.”

ESV.1: With my voice I cry out to the LORD; with my voice I plead for mercy to the LORD.
ESV.2: I pour out my complaint before him; I tell my trouble before him.
ESV.3: When my spirit faints within me, you know my way! In the path where I walk they have hidden a trap for me.
ESV.4: Look to the right and see: there is none who takes notice of me; no refuge remains to me; no one cares for my soul.
ESV.5: I cry to you, O LORD; I say, “You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.”
ESV.6: Attend to my cry, for I am brought very low! Deliver me from my persecutors, for they are too strong for me!
ESV.7: Bring me out of prison, that I may give thanks to your name! The righteous will surround me, for you will deal bountifully with me.

NASB.1: Maskil of David, when he was in the cave. A Prayer. I cry aloud with my voice to the LORD; I make supplication with my voice to the LORD.
NASB.2: I pour out my complaint before Him; I declare my trouble before Him.
NASB.3: When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, You knew my path. In the way where I walk They have hidden a trap for me.
NASB.4: Look to the right and see; For there is no one who regards me; There is no escape for me; No one cares for my soul.
NASB.5: I cried out to You, O LORD; I said, 'You are my refuge, My portion in the land of the living.
NASB.6: 'Give heed to my cry, For I am brought very low; Deliver me from my persecutors, For they are too strong for me.
NASB.7: 'Bring my soul out of prison, So that I may give thanks to Your name; The righteous will surround me, For You will deal bountifully with me.'

NLT.1:
I cry out to the LORD;
I plead for the LORD’s mercy.
NLT.2:
I pour out my complaints before him
and tell him all my troubles.
NLT.3:
When I am overwhelmed,
you alone know the way I should turn.
Wherever I go,
my enemies have set traps for me.
NLT.4:
I look for someone to come and help me,
but no one gives me a passing thought!
No one will help me;
no one cares a bit what happens to me.
NLT.5:
Then I pray to you, O LORD.
I say, “You are my place of refuge.
You are all I really want in life.
NLT.6:
Hear my cry,
for I am very low.
Rescue me from my persecutors,
for they are too strong for me.
NLT.7:
Bring me out of prison
so I can thank you.
The godly will crowd around me,
for you are good to me.”

WEB.1: A contemplation by David, when he was in the cave. A Prayer. I cry with my voice to Yahweh. With my voice, I ask Yahweh for mercy.
WEB.2: I pour out my complaint before him. I tell him my troubles.
WEB.3: When my spirit was overwhelmed within me, you knew my route. On the path in which I walk, they have hidden a snare for me.
WEB.4: Look on my right, and see; for there is no one who is concerned for me. Refuge has fled from me. No one cares for my soul.
WEB.5: I cried to you, Yahweh. I said, “You are my refuge, my portion in the land of the living.”
WEB.6: Listen to my cry, for I am in desperate need. deliver me from my persecutors, For they are stronger than me.
WEB.7: Bring my soul out of prison, that I may give thanks to your name. The righteous will surround me, for you will be good to me.

CSB17.1:
A of David. When he was in the cave. ⓗ A prayer. [1] I cry aloud to the LORD; ⓘ
I plead aloud to the LORD for mercy. ⓙ
CSB17.2:
I pour out my complaint before him;
I reveal my trouble to him. ⓚ
CSB17.3:
Although my spirit is weak within me,
you know my way. ⓛ
Along this path I travel
they have hidden a trap for me. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
Look to the right and see: [1]
no one stands up for me;
there is no refuge for me;
no one cares about me. ⓝ
CSB17.5:
I cry to you, LORD;
I say, “You are my shelter,
my portion in the land of the living.” ⓐ
CSB17.6:
Listen to my cry,
for I am very weak. ⓑ
Rescue me from those who pursue me,
for they are too strong for me. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
Free me from prison
so that I can praise your name.
The righteous will gather around me
because you deal generously with me. ⓓ

AMP.1:
A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem, of David; when he was in the cave. A Prayer. I cry aloud with my voice to the LORD;
I make supplication with my voice to the LORD.
AMP.2:
I pour out my complaint before Him;
I declare my trouble before Him.
AMP.3:
When my spirit was overwhelmed and weak within me [wrapped in darkness],
You knew my path.
In the way where I walk
They have hidden a trap for me.
AMP.4:
Look to the right [the point of attack] and see;
For there is no one who has regard for me [to act in my favor].
Escape has failed me and I have nowhere to run;
No one cares about my life.
AMP.5:
I cried out to You, O LORD;
I said, "You are my refuge,
My portion in the land of the living.
AMP.6:
"Give attention to my cry,
For I am brought very low;
Rescue me from my persecutors,
For they are stronger than I.
AMP.7:
"Bring my soul out of prison (adversity),
So that I may give thanks and praise Your name;
The righteous will surround me [in triumph],
For You will look after me."

MSG.1:
A David prayer—when he was in the cave.
I cry out loudly to God,
loudly I plead with God for mercy.
MSG.2:
I spill out all my complaints before him,
and spell out my troubles in detail:
MSG.3:
"As I sink in despair, my spirit ebbing away,
you know how I'm feeling,
Know the danger I'm in,
the traps hidden in my path.
MSG.4:
Look right, look left—
there's not a soul who cares what happens!
I'm up against it, with no exit—
bereft, left alone.
MSG.5:
I cry out, God, call out:
'You're my last chance, my only hope for life!'
MSG.6:
Oh listen, please listen;
I've never been this low.
Rescue me from those who are hunting me down;
I'm no match for them.
MSG.7:
Get me out of this dungeon
so I can thank you in public.
Your people will form a circle around me
and you'll bring me showers of blessing!"

NIV.1:
Psalm 142
A maskil of David. When he was in the cave. A prayer.
I cry aloud to the Lord;
I lift up my voice to the Lord for mercy.
NIV.2:
I pour out my complaint before him;
before him I tell my trouble.
NIV.3:
When my spirit grows faint within me,
it is you who know my way.
In the path where I walk
men have hidden a snare for me.
NIV.4:
Look to my right and see;
no one is concerned for me.
I have no refuge;
no one cares for my life.
NIV.5:
I cry to you, O Lord;
I say, “You are my refuge,
my portion in the land of the living.”
NIV.6:
Listen to my cry,
for I am in desperate need;
rescue me from those who pursue me,
for they are too strong for me.
NIV.7:
Set me free from my prison,
that I may praise your name.
Then the righteous will gather about me
because of your goodness to me.

NRSVCE.1:
With my voice I cry to the LORD;
with my voice I make supplication to the LORD.
NRSVCE.2:
I pour out my complaint before him;
I tell my trouble before him.
NRSVCE.3:
When my spirit is faint,
you know my way.
In the path where I walk
they have hidden a trap for me.
NRSVCE.4:
Look on my right hand and see —
there is no one who takes notice of me;
no refuge remains to me;
no one cares for me.
NRSVCE.5:
I cry to you, O LORD;
I say, “You are my refuge,
my portion in the land of the living.”
NRSVCE.6:
Give heed to my cry,
for I am brought very low.
Save me from my persecutors,
for they are too strong for me.
NRSVCE.7:
Bring me out of prison,
so that I may give thanks to your name.
The righteous will surround me,
for you will deal bountifully with me.

NET.1: To the LORD I cry out; to the LORD I plead for mercy.
NET.2: I pour out my lament before him; I tell him about my troubles.
NET.3: Even when my strength leaves me, you watch my footsteps. In the path where I walk they have hidden a trap for me.
NET.4: Look to the right and see! No one cares about me. I have nowhere to run; no one is concerned about my life.
NET.5: I cry out to you, O LORD; I say, “You are my shelter, my security in the land of the living.”
NET.6: Listen to my cry for help, for I am in serious trouble! Rescue me from those who chase me, for they are stronger than I am.
NET.7: Free me from prison, that I may give thanks to your name. Because of me the godly will assemble, for you will vindicate me.
